title: clusia pseudomangle
intro: Clusia pseudomangle, also known as the Amazon water lily or water poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the Clusiaceae family, characterized by its unique ability to grow on water and thrive in aquatic environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Clusia pseudomangle are said to include pain relief, wound healing, and protection against infections, as well as potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal benefits.
